title: eschscholzia fumariifolia
intro: Eschscholzia fumariifolia, also known as the California desert poppy or golden poppy, is a species of flowering plant in the poppy family, Papaveraceae. This plant is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, and its bright orange-yellow flowers have been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The medicinal properties of Eschscholzia fumariifolia include its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which have been used to treat a range of health issues, including pain, anxiety, and insomnia. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve sleep quality.
